scenario.context: Station 04 of the day run: deepen algorithms, recursion, and sorting.
scenario.mission: You design robust algorithms, validate recursion, and choose suitable sorting methods.

constraints: Choose the method according to context

customerRequest.subject: Station 04: Safeguard algorithmics
customerRequest.body: Please validate the algorithm design for load cases and document the choice of method.

questions.title: Algorithm starting point
questions.prompt: What comes first in a stable algorithm design?

options.text: Clarify problem, inputs, and edge cases
options.rationale: Creates a sound basis for the design.

feedback.correct: Correct: First clarify the problem and edge cases.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without problem clarity the design becomes messy.

questions.title: Make recursion robust
questions.prompt: Which points are especially important in recursion? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Plan test cases for boundary values

feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please pay more attention to termination, reduction, and boundary tests.

questions.title: Justify a sorting method
questions.prompt: Put the steps for deciding on a sorting method in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: The choice of method is justified cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please evaluate the data profile before implementation.

questions.title: Add processing times
questions.prompt: Three algorithm runs take 18 ms, 21 ms, and 24 ms. What is the total time?

questions.title: Explain method choice
questions.prompt: Explain the choice of sorting method for a real load profile in 4-6 sentences.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear link to the data profile and runtime impact.

feedback.correct: Correct: The choice is professionally traceable.
feedback.wrong: Please connect data profile, runtime, and method more clearly.
